principle: External audience outcomes are strategic ambitions, not directly executable agent goals.

agent_goal_policy.forbidden_completion_claim: Do not treat audience behavior, platform reach, or viral distribution as directly completed by an agent.

campaigns.title: One Million Views By End Of 2026
campaigns.unresolved_tension: ph-civ must seek public reach without letting view-count ambition outrun source-disciplined educational trust.
campaigns.agent_executable_translation: Build the public-growth system that makes the ambition plausible: polished repo narrative, chapter index, shareable assets, contribution paths, analytics definitions, distribution calendar, and weekly review loop.

first_live_wedge.title: Launch the Volume I literary spine as the first shareable route
first_live_wedge.readiness_question: What must be true before this route deserves audience growth rather than merely being technically shareable?
scope: view-count definition for the first public route
first_live_wedge.done_when: A human-approved launch packet can point readers to the Homer-to-Tolstoy route, explain why it is the Volume I spine, and state how views will be counted without claiming views have already been earned.

measurable_agent_outputs: README and public contract explain the two-volume ph-civ artifact within one screen
chapter and volume routes are easy to share
analytics plan defines what counts as a view across GitHub, web, video, social, and document surfaces
distribution calendar converts the target into weekly and monthly milestones
launch copy and visual assets are staged for human approval
